🔬 Science

Information and Communication Technology

Explore the world of computers, internet, and modern communication!

📚 Unit 2 ⏱️ 6 min read

Unit 2: Information and Communication Technology (ICT)

ICT stands for Information and Communication Technology. It includes all technologies used to handle information and communicate.

What is ICT?

ICT is the use of computers, internet, and other technologies to:

  • Store information
  • Process information
  • Share information
  • Communicate with others

Example: Using a smartphone to send a message, using a computer to write a document, or using the internet to watch a video.

Evolution of Communication

Humans have developed better ways to communicate over time.

EraMethod of Communication
Ancient timesDrum beats, smoke signals, messengers on foot
Middle AgesCarrier pigeons, written letters
1800sTelegraph, telephone
1900sRadio, television, early computers
Modern timesMobile phones, internet, video calls

💡 Did you know? The first telephone was invented by Alexander Graham Bell in 1876.

Components of ICT

ICT has four main parts:

ComponentWhat it includesExamples
HardwarePhysical parts of a computerCPU, monitor, keyboard, mouse
SoftwarePrograms that run on computersWindows, MS Office, games
DataInformation being processedText, numbers, images, videos
NetworkConnecting devices togetherInternet, Wi-Fi, Bluetooth

Computer Hardware

Basic Parts of a Computer

PartFunctionImage Description
CPU (Central Processing Unit)The “brain” of the computerA small box that processes information
MonitorDisplays informationLike a TV screen
KeyboardTypes letters and numbersHas many buttons (keys)
MousePoints and clicks on the screenA small device you move with your hand
SpeakersProduces soundPlays music and audio
PrinterPrints on paperMakes physical copies

Input and Output Devices

TypeWhat it doesExamples
Input DeviceSends information INTO the computerKeyboard, mouse, microphone, scanner
Output DeviceShows information FROM the computerMonitor, printer, speakers

💡 Remember: Input goes in, output comes out!

Computer Software

Software is a set of instructions that tells the computer what to do.

Type of SoftwareExamplesUse
Operating SystemWindows, macOS, LinuxRuns the computer
Word ProcessorMS Word, Google DocsWriting letters, stories
SpreadsheetMS Excel, Google SheetsMaking tables, calculations
PresentationMS PowerPoint, Google SlidesMaking slideshows
Web BrowserChrome, Firefox, SafariBrowsing the internet
GamesMinecraft, educational gamesEntertainment and learning

The Internet

The internet is a global network of computers connected together.

Uses of the Internet

UseExample
CommunicationEmail, WhatsApp, video calls
Information searchGoogle, Wikipedia
EntertainmentYouTube, Netflix, online games
EducationOnline classes, educational websites
ShoppingAmazon, Daraz, Flipkart
BankingOnline bill payments, money transfer

Internet Safety Rules

RuleWhy it matters
Do not share personal informationProtect your privacy
Do not talk to strangers onlineSome people may not be honest
Ask a parent before downloadingSome files have viruses
Do not believe everything you seeSome information is false
Limit screen timeToo much screen time harms eyes

⚠️ Warning: Never share your password with anyone except your parents!

Computer Networks

A network is two or more computers connected together.

Type of NetworkSizeExample
LAN (Local Area Network)Small (one building)Computers in a school lab
WAN (Wide Area Network)Large (cities, countries)The internet
Wi-FiWireless networkHome internet connection

Modern Communication Technologies

TechnologyWhat it doesExample
Mobile phoneCall, text, use appsSmartphone
SatelliteSends signals around EarthTV broadcast, GPS
Video conferencingSee and talk to people far awayZoom, Google Meet
Social mediaShare posts and messagesFacebook, Instagram, TikTok
Cloud storageSave files onlineGoogle Drive, Dropbox

Advantages and Disadvantages of ICT

Advantages ✅

AdvantageExplanation
Fast communicationSend messages instantly
Easy access to informationFind anything online
Saves time and moneyOnline shopping, online banking
Education from homeOnline classes, tutorials
EntertainmentMovies, music, games

Disadvantages ❌

DisadvantageExplanation
CyberbullyingPeople being mean online
Privacy risksPersonal information can be stolen
AddictionSpending too much time online
Fake newsWrong information spreads easily
Health problemsEye strain, less physical activity

Try It Yourself!

Exercise 1: Fill in the Blanks

  1. ICT stands for ____________ and ____________ Technology.
  2. The “brain” of the computer is the ____________.
  3. A ____________ is used to type letters and numbers.
  4. The internet is a global ____________ of computers.
  5. ____________ is a web browser.

Exercise 2: Input or Output?

Write “Input” or “Output” for each device.

  1. Keyboard ______
  2. Monitor ______
  3. Mouse ______
  4. Printer ______
  5. Microphone ______

Exercise 3: Match the Pair

Column AColumn B
CPUa) Shows pictures
Monitorb) Points and clicks
Mousec) Brain of computer
Printerd) Makes paper copies
Speakerse) Produces sound

Exercise 4: Internet Safety

Write two internet safety rules you should follow.

Answer Key

Exercise 1: 1. Information, Communication; 2. CPU; 3. keyboard; 4. network; 5. Chrome (or Firefox, Safari)

Exercise 2: 1. Input, 2. Output, 3. Input, 4. Output, 5. Input

Exercise 3: CPU→c, Monitor→a, Mouse→b, Printer→d, Speakers→e

Great work! ICT is changing the world. Use technology wisely and safely!

🎯
